children: U.S. STOCKS SEESAW ON CPI, BRACE FOR BANK EARNINGS, PPI
The main U.S. indexes gyrated on Thursday before managing to end the session barely changed.

Indeed, the market showed resilience despite hotter-than-expected CPI data and signs of labor market strength, which could serve to dampen hopes for early interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year.
After an opening upside push, the S&P 500 index
quickly turned south, and slid nearly 1% into a late-morning low.
However, the benchmark index then reversed, and finished down 0.07%. The DJI
and Nasdaq Composite
followed similar paths, but the blue-chip Dow edged into the green at the close, while the tech-laden Nasdaq finished unchanged.
Most S&P 500 sectors finished down with utilities
, off more than 2%, taking the biggest hit.
Tech
and energy
both posted slight gains.
Under the surface, chip stocks
were an outperformer, rising about 0.5%.
Banks were on the losing side before the kickoff of Q4 earnings season on Friday. The S&P 500 banks index (.SPXBK) fell about 0.9%, while the KBW regional banking index (.KRX) lost about 1.24%.
Bank of America
, Citigroup
, JP Morgan
and Wells Fargo
are due to report Friday morning.
December PPI data is also due Friday morning. Expectations call for a month-over-month read of 0.1% vs a 0.0% prior print and a year-over-year read of 1.3% vs 0.9% last month.
Month-over-month ex-food/energy is expected up 0.2% vs a 0.0% prior print and 1.9% year-over-year vs 2.0% last month.
Here is your closing snapshot:
(Terence Gabriel)

title: U.S. stocks seesaw on CPI, brace for bank earnings, PPI
